ip_outcome_note: Species name printed on card face; PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-42) confirms name=Simipour, nationalPokedexNumbers=[516], evolvesFrom=Panpour. Depicted blue monkey with large curly topknot seated in pool matches canonical Simipour appearance exactly.

trivia_bullets.text: Darkness Ablaze is the 3rd Sword & Shield expansion, released August 14, 2020; 189 base-set cards (201 with secret rares), anchored by Charizard VMAX and Eternatus VMAX as the primary chase cards. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3 set metadata]

Simipour is National Pokedex no. 516, a Water-type Stage 1 introduced in Generation V (Pokemon Black and White, 2010), evolving from Panpour via Water Stone — one of three elemental monkey evolutions (Simisage / Simisear / Simipour) unlocked by Leaf, Fire, and Water Stones respectively. Stone evolution means the evolved form learns no moves by level-up, making timing of the Stone use important. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-42; PokemonDB: Simipour]

The elemental monkey trio (Pansage / Pansear / Panpour and their evolutions) is widely understood to reference the Three Wise Monkeys — the Japanese pictorial maxim of see-no-evil / hear-no-evil / speak-no-evil. Panpour / Simipour maps to Mizaru (see-no-evil): Panpour's closed-eye design encodes the covered-eyes gesture. Pansear represents Kikazaru (hear-no-evil) and Pansage represents Iwazaru (speak-no-evil). [Bulbapedia: Elemental monkeys, via WebSearch snippet; name-rater.tumblr.com: Mizaru/Kikazaru/Iwazaru mapping]
Flavor text: "It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail." — printed on DAA-042. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-42]

Art by Shigenori Negishi. The card art shows Simipour seated in a rocky tide-pool — a direct visual expression of the flavor text's clean-water habitat preference and the Mizaru see-no-evil serenity register (relaxed seated pose, forward-facing, calm). [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-42 artist field]
